groups.name: Correctness
groups.description: Wrong, unsafe, or structurally invalid code patterns.

groups.name: Anti-pattern
groups.description: Discouraged patterns that often lead to bugs or confusing behavior.

groups.name: Effect-native
groups.description: Prefer Effect-native APIs and abstractions when available.

groups.name: Style
groups.description: Cleanup, consistency, and idiomatic Effect code.

presets.name: effect-native
presets.description: Enable all Effect-native diagnostics at warning level.

rules.name: anyUnknownInErrorContext
rules.description: Detects 'any' or 'unknown' types in Effect error or requirements channels

diagnostics.text:
This has unknown in the requirements channel and any in the error channel which is not recommended.
Only service identifiers should appear in the requirements channel.
Having an unknown or any error type is not useful. Consider instead using specific error types baked by Data.TaggedError for example.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

This has unknown in the requirements channel which is not recommended.
Only service identifiers should appear in the requirements channel.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

This has any in the error channel which is not recommended.
Having an unknown or any error type is not useful. Consider instead using specific error types baked by Data.TaggedError for example.    effect(anyUnknownInErrorContext)

rules.name: classSelfMismatch
rules.description: Ensures Self type parameter matches the class name in Service/Tag/Schema classes

diagnostics.text: The `Self` type parameter for this class should be `InvalidContextTag`.    effect(classSelfMismatch)

rules.name: duplicatePackage
rules.description: Detects when multiple versions of the same Effect package are loaded

rules.name: effectFnImplicitAny
rules.description: Mirrors noImplicitAny for unannotated Effect.fn and Effect.fnUntraced callback parameters when no outer contextual function type exists

preview.sourceText:
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

export const preview = Effect.fn("preview")((input) => Effect.succeed(input))

diagnostics.text: Parameter `input` implicitly has type `any` in `Effect.fn`, `Effect.fnUntraced`, or `Effect.fnUntracedEager`. No parameter type is available from an explicit annotation or contextual function type.    effect(effectFnImplicitAny)

rules.name: floatingEffect
rules.description: Ensures Effects are yielded or assigned to variables, not left floating

preview.sourceText:
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

Effect.log("forgotten")

diagnostics.text: This Effect value is neither yielded nor used in an assignment.    effect(floatingEffect)

rules.name: genericEffectServices
rules.description: Prevents services with type parameters that cannot be discriminated at runtime

diagnostics.text: Effect Services with type parameters are not supported because they cannot be properly discriminated at runtime, which may cause unexpected behavior.    effect(genericEffectServices)

rules.name: missingEffectContext
rules.description: Reports missing service requirements in Effect context channel

diagnostics.text: This Effect requires a service that is missing from the expected Effect context: `Db`.    effect(missingEffectContext)

rules.name: missingEffectError
rules.description: Reports missing error types in Effect error channel

diagnostics.text: Missing 'Boom' in the expected Effect errors.    effect(missingEffectError)

rules.name: missingLayerContext
rules.description: Reports missing service requirements in Layer context channel

diagnostics.text: Missing 'A' in the expected Layer context.    effect(missingLayerContext)

rules.name: missingReturnYieldStar
rules.description: Suggests using 'return yield*' for Effects with never success for better type narrowing

diagnostics.text: This Effect never succeeds; using `return yield*` preserves a definitive generator exit point for type narrowing and tooling support.    effect(missingReturnYieldStar)

rules.name: missingStarInYieldEffectGen
rules.description: Enforces using 'yield*' instead of 'yield' when yielding Effects in generators

diagnostics.text: This uses `yield` for an `Effect` value. `yield*` is the Effect-aware form in this context.    effect(missingStarInYieldEffectGen)
This uses `yield` for an `Effect` value. `yield*` is the Effect-aware form in this context.    effect(missingStarInYieldEffectGen)

rules.name: nonObjectEffectServiceType
rules.description: Ensures Effect.Service types are objects, not primitives

diagnostics.text: `Effect.Service` is declared with a primitive service type. `Effect.Service` models object-shaped services; primitive values use `Context.Tag` or `Effect.Tag` directly.    effect(nonObjectEffectServiceType)

rules.name: outdatedApi
rules.description: Detects usage of APIs that have been removed or renamed in Effect v4

diagnostics.text: This project targets Effect v4, but this code uses Effect v3 APIs. The referenced API belongs to the v3 surface rather than the configured v4 surface.    effect(outdatedApi)
This project targets Effect v4, but this code uses the Effect v3 API `runtime`. The referenced API belongs to the v3 surface rather than the configured v4 surface.    effect(outdatedApi)

rules.name: outdatedEffectCodegen
rules.description: Detects when generated code is outdated and needs to be regenerated

diagnostics.text: Codegen accessors result is outdated    effect(outdatedEffectCodegen)

rules.name: overriddenSchemaConstructor
rules.description: Prevents overriding constructors in Schema classes which breaks decoding behavior

diagnostics.text: This Schema subclass defines its own constructor. For Schema classes, constructor overrides break decoding behavior for the class shape. Custom construction can be expressed through a static `new` method instead.    effect(overriddenSchemaConstructor)

rules.name: unsupportedServiceAccessors
rules.description: Warns about service accessors that need codegen due to generic/complex signatures

diagnostics.text: Even if accessors are enabled, accessors for 'get' won't be available because the signature have generic type parameters or multiple call signatures.    effect(unsupportedServiceAccessors)

rules.name: catchUnfailableEffect
rules.description: Warns when using error handling on Effects that never fail (error type is 'never')

diagnostics.text: The previous Effect does not fail, so this error-handling branch will never run.    effect(catchUnfailableEffect)

rules.name: effectFnIife
rules.description: Effect.fn or Effect.fnUntraced is called as an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression). Use Effect.gen instead.

diagnostics.text: `Effect.fn` returns a reusable function that can take arguments, but it is invoked immediately here. `Effect.gen` represents the immediate-use form for this pattern with `Effect.withSpan("preview")` piped at the end to maintain tracing spans.    effect(effectFnIife)

rules.name: effectGenUsesAdapter
rules.description: Warns when using the deprecated adapter parameter in Effect.gen

diagnostics.text: The adapter of Effect.gen is not required anymore, it is now just an alias of pipe.    effect(effectGenUsesAdapter)

rules.name: effectInFailure
rules.description: Warns when an Effect is used inside an Effect failure channel

diagnostics.text: The error channel contains an Effect (Effect<void, never, never>). Putting Effect computations in the failure channel is not intended; keep only failure types there.    effect(effectInFailure)
The error channel contains an Effect (Effect<void, never, never>). Putting Effect computations in the failure channel is not intended; keep only failure types there.    effect(effectInFailure)

rules.name: effectInVoidSuccess
rules.description: Detects nested Effects in void success channels that may cause unexecuted effects

diagnostics.text: There is a nested 'Effect<void, never, never>' in the 'void' success channel, beware that this could lead to nested Effect<Effect<...>> that won't be executed.    effect(effectInVoidSuccess)

rules.name: globalErrorInEffectCatch
rules.description: Warns when catch callbacks return global Error type instead of typed errors

diagnostics.text: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryPromise` returns the global `Error` type. Untagged errors merge together in the Effect error channel and lose type-level distinction; a tagged error preserves that distinction and can wrap the original error in a `cause` property.    effect(globalErrorInEffectCatch)

rules.name: globalErrorInEffectFailure
rules.description: Warns when the global Error type is used in an Effect failure channel

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.fail(new Error("boom"))

diagnostics.text: Global 'Error' loses type safety as untagged errors merge together in the Effect failure channel. Consider using a tagged error and optionally wrapping the original in a 'cause' property.    effect(globalErrorInEffectFailure)

rules.name: layerMergeAllWithDependencies
rules.description: Detects interdependencies in Layer.mergeAll calls where one layer provides a service that another layer requires

diagnostics.text: This layer provides A which is required by another layer in the same Layer.mergeAll call. Layer.mergeAll creates layers in parallel, so dependencies between layers will not be satisfied. Consider moving this layer into a Layer.provideMerge after the Layer.mergeAll.    effect(layerMergeAllWithDependencies)

rules.name: lazyPromiseInEffectSync
rules.description: Warns when Effect.sync lazily returns a Promise instead of using an async Effect constructor

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.sync(() => Promise.resolve(1))

diagnostics.text: This `Effect.sync` thunk returns a Promise. Use `Effect.promise` or `Effect.tryPromise` to represent async work.    effect(lazyPromiseInEffectSync)

rules.name: leakingRequirements
rules.description: Detects implementation services leaked in service methods

diagnostics.text:
Methods of this Service require `FileSystem` from every caller.

The requirement becomes part of the public service surface instead of remaining internal to Layer implementation.

Resolve these dependencies at Layer creation and provide them to each method, so the service's type reflects its purpose, not its implementation.

To suppress this diagnostic for specific dependency types that are intentionally passed through (e.g., HttpServerRequest), add `@effect-leakable-service` JSDoc to their interface declarations (e.g., the `FileSystem` interface), or to this service by adding a `@effect-expect-leaking FileSystem` JSDoc.

More info and examples at https://effect.website/docs/requirements-management/layers/#avoiding-requirement-leakage    effect(leakingRequirements)

rules.name: multipleEffectProvide
rules.description: Warns against chaining Effect.provide calls which can cause service lifecycle issues

diagnostics.text: This expression chains multiple `Effect.provide` calls. Providing Layers in multiple calls in a chain can break service lifecycle behavior compared with a single combined provide with merged layers.    effect(multipleEffectProvide)

rules.name: returnEffectInGen
rules.description: Warns when returning an Effect in a generator causes nested Effect<Effect<...>>

diagnostics.text: This generator returns an Effect-able value directly, which produces a nested `Effect<Effect<...>>`. If the intended result is the inner Effect value, `return yield*` represents that form.    effect(returnEffectInGen)

rules.name: runEffectInsideEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Runtime or Effect.run*With methods instead of Effect.run* inside Effect contexts

diagnostics.text: `Effect.runSync` is called inside an Effect with a separate services invocation. In this context, child Effects run with the surrounding services, which can be accessed through `Effect.services` and `Effect.runSyncWith`.    effect(runEffectInsideEffect)

rules.name: schemaSyncInEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Effect-based Schema methods instead of sync methods inside Effect generators

diagnostics.text: `Schema.decodeSync` is used inside an Effect generator. `Schema.decode` preserves the typed Effect error channel for this operation without throwing.    effect(schemaSyncInEffect)

rules.name: scopeInLayerEffect
rules.description: Suggests using Layer.scoped instead of Layer.effect when Scope is in requirements

diagnostics.text: This layer construction leaves `Scope` in the requirement set. The scoped API removes `Scope` from the resulting requirements.    effect(scopeInLayerEffect)

rules.name: strictEffectProvide
rules.description: Warns when using Effect.provide with layers outside of application entry points

diagnostics.text: Effect.provide with a Layer should only be used at application entry points. If this is an entry point, you can safely disable this diagnostic. Otherwise, using Effect.provide may break scope lifetimes. Compose all layers at your entry point and provide them at once.    effect(strictEffectProvide)

rules.name: tryCatchInEffectGen
rules.description: Discourages try/catch in Effect generators in favor of Effect error handling

diagnostics.text: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag).    effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)

rules.name: unknownInEffectCatch
rules.description: Warns when catch callbacks return unknown instead of typed errors

diagnostics.text: The `catch` callback in `Effect.tryPromise` returns `unknown`, so the Effect error type stays untyped. A specific typed error preserves error-channel information, for example by narrowing the value or wrapping it in `Data.TaggedError`.    effect(unknownInEffectCatch)

rules.name: asyncFunction
rules.description: Warns when declaring async functions and suggests using Effect values and Effect.gen for async control flow

diagnostics.text: This code declares an async function, consider representing this async control flow with Effect values and `Effect.gen`.    effect(asyncFunction)

rules.name: cryptoRandomUUID
rules.description: Warns when using crypto.randomUUID() outside Effect generators instead of the Effect Random module, which uses Effect-injected randomness rather than the crypto module behind the scenes

preview.sourceText: export const preview = crypto.randomUUID()

diagnostics.text: This code uses `crypto.randomUUID()`, prefer the Effect `Random` module instead because it uses Effect-injected randomness rather than the `crypto` module behind the scenes.    effect(cryptoRandomUUID)

rules.name: cryptoRandomUUIDInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using crypto.randomUUID() inside Effect generators instead of the Effect Random module, which uses Effect-injected randomness rather than the crypto module behind the scenes

diagnostics.text: This Effect code uses `crypto.randomUUID()`, prefer the Effect `Random` module instead because it uses Effect-injected randomness rather than the `crypto` module behind the scenes.    effect(cryptoRandomUUIDInEffect)

rules.name: extendsNativeError
rules.description: Warns when a class directly extends the native Error class

preview.sourceText: export class PreviewError extends Error {}

diagnostics.text: This class extends the native `Error` type directly. Untagged native errors lose distinction in the Effect failure channel.    effect(extendsNativeError)

rules.name: globalConsole
rules.description: Warns when using console methods outside Effect generators instead of Effect.log/Logger

preview.sourceText: console.log("preview")

diagnostics.text: This code uses `console.log`, the corresponding Effect logging API is `Effect.log or Logger`.    effect(globalConsole)

rules.name: globalConsoleInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using console methods inside Effect generators instead of Effect.log/Logger

diagnostics.text: This Effect code uses `console.log`, logging in Effect code is represented through `Effect.log or Logger`.    effect(globalConsoleInEffect)

rules.name: globalDate
rules.description: Warns when using Date.now() or new Date() outside Effect generators instead of Clock/DateTime

preview.sourceText: export const preview = Date.now()

diagnostics.text: This code uses `Date.now()`, time access is represented through `Clock` from Effect.    effect(globalDate)

rules.name: globalDateInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using Date.now() or new Date() inside Effect generators instead of Clock/DateTime

diagnostics.text: This Effect code uses `Date.now()`, time access in Effect code is represented through `Clock` from Effect.    effect(globalDateInEffect)

rules.name: globalFetch
rules.description: Warns when using the global fetch function outside Effect generators instead of the Effect HTTP client

preview.sourceText: export const preview = fetch("https://example.com")

diagnostics.text: This code uses the global `fetch` function, HTTP requests are represented through `HttpClient` from `@effect/platform`.    effect(globalFetch)

rules.name: globalFetchInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using the global fetch function inside Effect generators instead of the Effect HTTP client

rules.name: globalRandom
rules.description: Warns when using Math.random() outside Effect generators instead of the Random service

preview.sourceText: export const preview = Math.random()

diagnostics.text: This code uses `Math.random()`, randomness is represented through the Effect `Random` service.    effect(globalRandom)

rules.name: globalRandomInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using Math.random() inside Effect generators instead of the Random service

diagnostics.text: This Effect code uses `Math.random()`, randomness is represented through the Effect `Random` service.    effect(globalRandomInEffect)

rules.name: globalTimers
rules.description: Warns when using setTimeout/setInterval outside Effect generators instead of Effect.sleep/Schedule

preview.sourceText: setTimeout(() => {}, 100)

diagnostics.text: This code uses `setTimeout`, the corresponding Effect timer API is `Effect.sleep or Schedule` from Effect.    effect(globalTimers)

rules.name: globalTimersInEffect
rules.description: Warns when using setTimeout/setInterval inside Effect generators instead of Effect.sleep/Schedule

diagnostics.text: This Effect code uses `setTimeout`, the corresponding timer API in this context is `Effect.sleep or Schedule` from Effect.    effect(globalTimersInEffect)

rules.name: instanceOfSchema
rules.description: Suggests using Schema.is instead of instanceof for Effect Schema types

preview.sourceText:
import { Schema } from "effect"

class User extends Schema.Class<User>("User")({ name: Schema.String }) {}
declare const value: unknown
export const preview = value instanceof User

diagnostics.text: This code uses `instanceof` with an Effect Schema type. `Schema.is` is the schema-aware runtime check for this case.    effect(instanceOfSchema)

rules.name: newPromise
rules.description: Warns when constructing promises with new Promise instead of using Effect APIs

preview.sourceText: export const preview = new Promise<number>((resolve) => resolve(1))

diagnostics.text: This code constructs `new Promise(...)`, prefer Effect APIs such as `Effect.async`, `Effect.promise`, or `Effect.tryPromise` instead of manual Promise construction.    effect(newPromise)

rules.name: nodeBuiltinImport
rules.description: Warns when importing Node.js built-in modules that have Effect-native counterparts

preview.sourceText:
import fs from "node:fs"

export const preview = fs.readFileSync

diagnostics.text: This module reference uses the `fs` module, the corresponding Effect API is `FileSystem` from `@effect/platform`.    effect(nodeBuiltinImport)

rules.name: preferSchemaOverJson
rules.description: Suggests using Effect Schema for JSON operations instead of JSON.parse/JSON.stringify which may throw

diagnostics.text: This code uses `JSON.parse` or `JSON.stringify`. Effect Schema provides Effect-aware APIs for JSON parsing and stringifying.    effect(preferSchemaOverJson)

rules.name: processEnv
rules.description: Warns when reading process.env outside Effect generators instead of using Effect Config

preview.sourceText:
/// <reference types="node" />

export const preview = process.env.PORT

diagnostics.text: This code reads from `process.env`, environment configuration is represented through `Config` from Effect.    effect(processEnv)

rules.name: processEnvInEffect
rules.description: Warns when reading process.env inside Effect generators instead of using Effect Config

diagnostics.text: This Effect code reads from `process.env`, environment configuration in Effect code is represented through `Config` from Effect.    effect(processEnvInEffect)

rules.name: catchAllToMapError
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.mapError instead of Effect.catchAll when the callback only wraps the error with Effect.fail

diagnostics.text: `Effect.mapError` expresses the same error-type transformation more directly than `Effect.catch` followed by `Effect.fail`.    effect(catchAllToMapError)

rules.name: deterministicKeys
rules.description: Enforces deterministic naming for service/tag/error identifiers based on class names

diagnostics.text: This key does not match the deterministic key for this declaration. The expected key is `@effect/harness-effect-v4/examples/diagnostics/deterministicKeys_preview/RenamedService`.    effect(deterministicKeys)

rules.name: effectFnOpportunity
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.fn for functions that returns an Effect

diagnostics.text: This expression can be rewritten in the reusable function form `Effect.fn(function*() { ... })`.    effect(effectFnOpportunity)

rules.name: effectMapVoid
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.asVoid instead of Effect.map(() => void 0), Effect.map(() => undefined), or Effect.map(() => {})

diagnostics.text: This expression discards the success value through mapping. `Effect.asVoid` represents that form directly.    effect(effectMapVoid)

rules.name: effectSucceedWithVoid
rules.description: Suggests using Effect.void instead of Effect.succeed(undefined) or Effect.succeed(void 0)

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.succeed(undefined)

diagnostics.text: `Effect.void` represents the same outcome as `Effect.succeed(undefined)` or `Effect.succeed(void 0)`.    effect(effectSucceedWithVoid)

rules.name: importFromBarrel
rules.description: Suggests importing from specific module paths instead of barrel exports

preview.sourceText:
import { Effect } from "effect"

export const preview = Effect.void

diagnostics.text: Importing from barrel module effect is not allowed.    effect(importFromBarrel)

rules.name: missedPipeableOpportunity
rules.description: Enforces the use of pipeable style for nested function calls

diagnostics.text: This nested call structure has a pipeable form. `Schema.decodeEffect(User)({ id: 1 }).pipe(...)` represents the same call sequence in pipe style and may be easier to read.    effect(missedPipeableOpportunity)

rules.name: missingEffectServiceDependency
rules.description: Checks that Effect.Service dependencies satisfy all required layer inputs

diagnostics.text: Service 'Db' is required but not provided by dependencies    effect(missingEffectServiceDependency)

rules.name: redundantSchemaTagIdentifier
rules.description: Suggests removing redundant identifier argument when it equals the tag value in Schema.TaggedClass/TaggedError/TaggedRequest

diagnostics.text: Identifier 'Preview' is redundant since it equals the _tag value    effect(redundantSchemaTagIdentifier)

rules.name: schemaStructWithTag
rules.description: Suggests using Schema.TaggedStruct instead of Schema.Struct with _tag field

diagnostics.text: This `Schema.Struct` includes a `_tag` field. `Schema.TaggedStruct` is the tagged-struct form for this pattern and makes the tag optional in the constructor.    effect(schemaStructWithTag)

rules.name: schemaUnionOfLiterals
rules.description: Simplifies Schema.Union of multiple Schema.Literal calls into single Schema.Literal

preview.sourceText:
import * as Schema from "effect/Schema"

export const preview = Schema.Union(Schema.Literal("a"), Schema.Literal("b"))

diagnostics.text: This `Schema.Union` contains multiple `Schema.Literal` members and can be simplified to a single `Schema.Literal` call.    effect(schemaUnionOfLiterals)

rules.name: serviceNotAsClass
rules.description: Warns when ServiceMap.Service is used as a variable instead of a class declaration

preview.sourceText:
import { ServiceMap } from "effect"

export const Preview = ServiceMap.Service<{ port: number }>("Preview")

diagnostics.text: `ServiceMap.Service` is assigned to a variable here, but this API is intended for a class declaration shape such as `class Preview extends ServiceMap.Service<Preview, { port: number }>()("Preview") {}`.    effect(serviceNotAsClass)

rules.name: strictBooleanExpressions
rules.description: Enforces boolean types in conditional expressions for type safety

preview.sourceText:
declare const value: string | undefined
export const preview = value ? 1 : 0

diagnostics.text: Unexpected `string` type in condition, expected strictly a boolean instead.    effect(strictBooleanExpressions)
Unexpected `undefined` type in condition, expected strictly a boolean instead.    effect(strictBooleanExpressions)

rules.name: unnecessaryEffectGen
rules.description: Suggests removing Effect.gen when it contains only a single return statement

diagnostics.text: This Effect.gen contains a single return statement.    effect(unnecessaryEffectGen)

rules.name: unnecessaryFailYieldableError
rules.description: Suggests yielding yieldable errors directly instead of wrapping with Effect.fail

diagnostics.text: This `yield* Effect.fail(...)` passes a yieldable error value. `yield*` represents that value directly without wrapping it in `Effect.fail`.    effect(unnecessaryFailYieldableError)

rules.name: unnecessaryPipe
rules.description: Removes pipe calls with no arguments

preview.sourceText:
import { pipe } from "effect/Function"

export const preview = pipe(1)

diagnostics.text: This pipe call contains no arguments.    effect(unnecessaryPipe)

rules.name: unnecessaryPipeChain
rules.description: Simplifies chained pipe calls into a single pipe call

preview.sourceText:
import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"

export const preview = Effect.succeed(1).pipe(Effect.asVoid).pipe(Effect.as("done"))

diagnostics.text: This expression contains chained `pipe` calls that can be simplified to a single `pipe` call.    effect(unnecessaryPipeChain)
